ai_analysis.one_sentence_summary: Netcat policy tripped on adservice while the exported alert list is empty—open the full runtime view in ACS before you treat this as confirmed malicious activity.
ai_analysis.true_positive_rationale: The alert is ACTIVE and tied to a netcat policy, but this JSON export carries no violation lines; treat as a signal that still needs corroboration from Central.
ai_analysis.attack_vector: If netcat truly ran inside adservice, an attacker with foothold could probe internal ports or tunnel; if it is noise, wasting cycles still distracts responders.
ai_analysis.blast_radius: Contained to adservice unless combined with broader exec or credential theft findings from the same timeframe.
ai_analysis.prioritization_rationale: MEDIUM policy plus ambiguous evidence sits behind confirmed HIGH runtime issues (e.g., exec abuse) on the same workload.
recommended_actions: Review the live alert in ACS for process or network indicators backing the policy.
Remove nc from the container image if it is not required for health checks.
Cross-reference timeline with alert-03 exec events to see if the same operator ran both behaviors.
mitigation_strategies: Apply egress controls temporarily if you suspect active probing while you validate the detection.
